name: activemq
explanation: The activemq integration is a JMXFetch-backed check (spec inherits `instances/jmx`, `is_jmx: true`). JMX-based checks always need `host` + `port` and, in any production-shaped deployment, `user` + `password` — ActiveMQ's JMX agent enables authentication by default in modern releases via `jmx.access` / `jmx.password` files. Spec marks user/password as optional (so an unauthenticated localhost broker works), but per the procedure rubric, JMXFetch-based integrations are classified as impossible because credentials cannot be discovered from the wire even when the JMX port itself can be probed.
notes: ActiveMQ also has a sibling integration `activemq_xml` that scrapes the web console — different code path, different feasibility. This entry covers only the JMX-based `activemq` check.
